changes.rationale: Day-11 blocked 2 entries at conf 0.62 and 0.60; admitted entries cluster 0.65-0.72 (strategy hugs floor). Cumulative 11d net -₹2,566 dominated by single MAHABANK -₹3,516; ex-outlier +₹950 — core process roughly breakeven, earns room to test mid-conviction band. Day-11 risk_assessment_1530.json explicitly noted n=15 sample precondition met; deferred only by risk_monitor's Day-10 disciplined-venue rule, which the strategy review may bypass.
changes.revert_trigger: If win rate over Days 12-16 falls below 35% OR cumulative drawdown breaches 1% of capital from this point, revert to 0.65.

changes.rationale: Today's full-fill deployment 45.76% sat 19.24 pp under the 0.65 cap — cap is non-binding. Loosening to 0.75 unlocks zero immediate admission capacity but removes the psychological ceiling and brings overrides closer to the 0.80 base. Capital under-utilization is the dominant opportunity cost.
changes.revert_trigger: If deployment trends above 70% with concurrent loss streak (3+ consecutive negative-P&L days), tighten back to 0.65.

changes.rationale: CHENNPETRO entry 09:42 hit 'WARNING: SL too wide (4.0%). Max 4%' — the bot's intended 4% SL on a 5%-of-capital ATH breakout was a rational risk-budget choice (4% × 5% = 0.20% portfolio, well under 0.5% per-name cap). 3.5% override was artificially tight. 4.5% is closer to the 5% base while still preserving the post-Day-2 MAHABANK lesson that 6%+ SLs are unacceptable.
changes.revert_trigger: If a position with SL > 3.5% gets stopped out and contributes > 0.3% of portfolio loss, escalate review.

changes.rationale: Risk_monitor itself trial-loosened 0.22 → 0.28 at 09:00 today and revoked at 12:30 on a clean BFSI-restacking trigger (PNBHOUSING+BANDHANBNK+BAJFINANCE = 17.64%). Strategy-side review reinstates 0.28 because (a) BFSI's 17.64% is well clear of any cluster-stacking limit, (b) thematic plays (energy: ONGC/CHENNPETRO/GESHIP today) need room to express, (c) 0.22 vs 0.35 base was a 13 pp tightening that has been compounding for multiple days. 0.28 is the right middle ground.
changes.revert_trigger: If a single sector concentrates above 25% AND that sector underperforms the index over the next 3 days, snap back to 0.22.

changes.to: added — explicit numeric verification before invoking 'SL above/below CMP' as exit rationale
changes.rationale: NESTLEIND CLOSE 11:12 reasoning was numerically wrong ('SL 1442 ABOVE CMP 1449.10' but 1442 < 1449.10). Outcome was lucky +₹2,298 winner; reasoning was a perception error that should not be repeated.
changes.scope: next bot restart (system prompt built at boot)
changes.revert_trigger: n/a — prompt clarity addition, no downside

changes.to: added — exits within the same session as entry must cite post-entry-materialized facts, not pre-existing overhangs
changes.rationale: CHENNPETRO whipsaw 09:42 BUY → 10:44 SELL on overhangs that were public BEFORE entry. -3.7% intraday fade was within the 4% SL absorption budget. Either entry should have been NO_ACTION or position should have run to its real SL. Rule 13 already covers governance-overhang variant; today's data shows the failure mode generalizes.

changes.to: added — claimed R:R in reasoning must equal R:R implied by submitted stop_loss and target; final-step arithmetic check before emitting
changes.rationale: BAJFINANCE BUY 12:12 reasoning self-corrected mid-paragraph from R:R 1.4 → 1.6 'by adjusting target to 990' but submitted target was 985 (R:R 1.41) — sub-floor admission to overnight book. Guardrails check executed numbers, not prose.
